reports.suggestion_message:
to solve this problem, you can try the following approaches:

- ensure the maintainers know of this problem (e.g. creating a bug report if needed)
or even helping with a fix (e.g. by creating a pull request)
  - buf_redux@0.8.4
  - repository: https://github.com/abonander/buf_redux
  - detailed warning command: `cargo report future-incompatibilities --id 1 --package buf_redux@0.8.4`

  - multipart@0.18.0
  - repository: http://github.com/abonander/multipart
  - detailed warning command: `cargo report future-incompatibilities --id 1 --package multipart@0.18.0`

- use your own version of the dependency with the `[patch]` section in `Cargo.toml`
For more information, see:
https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/overriding-dependencies.html#the-patch-section

per_package.buf_redux@0.8.4:
The package `buf_redux v0.8.4` currently triggers the following future incompatibility lints:
> [1m[33mwarning[0m[1m: trailing semicolon in macro used in expression position[0m
>    [1m[94m--> [0m/Users/erickohlenberg/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/buf_redux-0.8.4/src/policy.rs:25:57
>     [1m[94m|[0m
> [1m[94m 25[0m [1m[94m|[0m     ($val:expr) => ( return $crate::policy::DoRead($val); );
>     [1m[94m|[0m                                                         [1m[33m^[0m
> [1m[94m...[0m
> [1m[94m118[0m [1m[94m|[0m         if buffer.len() >= self.0 { do_read!(false) }
>     [1m[94m|[0m                                     [1m[94m---------------[0m [1m[94min this macro invocation[0m
>     [1m[94m|[0m
>     [1m[94m= [0m[1mnote[0m: macro invocations at the end of a block are treated as expressions
>     [1m[94m= [0m[1mnote[0m: to ignore the value produced by the macro, add a semicolon after the invocation of `do_read`
>     [1m[94m= [0m[1mwarning[0m: this was previously accepted by the compiler but is being phased out; it will become a hard error in a future release!
>     [1m[94m= [0m[1mnote[0m: for more information, see issue #79813 <https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/79813>
>     [1m[94m= [0m[1mnote[0m: this warning originates in the macro `do_read` (in Nightly builds, run with -Z macro-backtrace for more info)
>
